prompt: I'm writing OCaml bindings to libpcapng, a network protocol parser that exposes a C++ iterator over frames. I want OCaml users to be able to run a piece of their own code for each frame as the iterator walks the collection. The collection has on the order of 800 million frames, and this path is performance-critical, so the per-frame step is hot. Please design the binding and give me the .mli interface file and the C stubs as a single self-contained unit.
